IDEAS Of THE


new CHARTER

pages.raw_text:
A. e. House, candidate for men,
bership on the commission that Is
to revise the city charter, Is a
believer In a progressive form Of
government. He resides at 1106
Spofford avenue and Is a railway
mail clerk. Mr.. House says:


am now and have been for
several years in favor of the COM
mission form of city government
Better results should be had where
authority and responsibility are
centralized in a few able men.


'But Of vastly greater import
ance than that responsibility be
fixed in five commissioners Is that
plain positive provisions should be
Incorporated in our charter that
shall forever keep the rens Of pow
er and final control in the hands
of the people. BY this l mean that
"a workable iniative referendum and
\.>}call must be devised that will
apply quickly. cheaply, and effec-
lively to all legislation and to the
tenure of office of elective officials.


positive safeguards should be
thrown around the granting or re-
newing Of franchises for publuic
service corporations.


"A merit system should be out
lined that will require all appoint
i've officials be selected with a
view solely to their fitness for the
work to be DONE"
